title: Fleet: audit + regenerate .env.template and child .env.* files via basher secrets
signal: Each spoke must audit its .env.template for completeness and correct annotations, then regenerate all child .env.* files. Issues are hidden from the user — broken op:// refs, missing variables, stale output targets, and un-annotated secrets produce silent failures at restore time. Every spoke agent must run this on themselves.
why_broadcast: Hidden misconfiguration in .env.template produces silent restore failures and incorrect child .env.* files across the fleet. No spoke has been systematically audited. Each agent must self-remediate using the basher secrets toolchain.

agent_instructions.summary: Run basher secrets audit on this spoke. Fix .env.template, then regenerate all .env.* output files.

commands: command -v basher && echo OK || echo MISSING
step_1_check_basher.if_missing: Source basher: source ~/.config/basher/bashrc.basher — if still missing, skip this signal and create a task to install basher first.

step_2_inventory.record: Note which .env.* files exist, whether template is present, and what format it's in (v1 onboarding vs v2 sectioned).

step_3_regenerate_template.description: Regenerate .env.template with latest basher format and agent instructions

if_template_missing.note: Will create a fresh template. Fill in variables afterward.

if_template_exists.note: Pass 'y' to confirm overwrite. This regenerates with the latest AGENT INSTRUCTIONS block, @output-* targets, and scans existing .env.* files to list them as @scan: entries.
if_template_exists.alternative: If echo pipe doesn't work in your shell context, run interactively: basher secrets init — then type 'y' at the overwrite prompt.

step_3_regenerate_template.verify: cat .env.template — confirm it has HOW TO USE IT section, VARIABLE ANNOTATIONS guide, and output target lines (@output-dev: etc.)

step_4_review_template.description: Review and correct the regenerated template

checks.check: All secret variables have op:// references (not empty strings)
checks.action: If empty strings found for #@secret vars: run 'basher secrets learn' to push values to 1Password and rewrite references
Add #@secret, #@plain, or #@optional to any unannotated variable lines
Update output targets if project deploys to Vercel, staging, or multiple envs — not just .env.local

checks.check: No hardcoded secrets (real values, not op:// refs)
checks.action: If real values found in #@secret lines: run 'basher secrets learn' to move them to 1Password immediately

checks.check: ENV sections exist if project has multiple environments
checks.action: If project has prod/staging .env files but template has no ENV sections: add sections manually or discuss with user

step_5_restore.description: Regenerate all child .env.* files from the corrected template
step_5_restore.note: Restores all ENV sections found in the template. Requires 1Password auth (op signin). If 1Password is not authenticated, run: eval $(op signin) first.
verify: ls -la .env* — confirm expected output files were created/updated
Check file timestamps — all should be freshly written
Spot-check one file: head -3 .env.local (or equivalent) — should contain actual values, not op:// refs

step_6_gitignore.command: cat .gitignore | grep -E '\.env' || echo 'WARNING: no .env gitignore rules'
step_6_gitignore.action: If missing: add appropriate .env.* patterns to .gitignore. The .env.template itself should NOT be gitignored — it's safe to commit.

actions: Create a signal lug confirming audit complete (or note any issues found)
If issues found that need human attention: create a task lug with findings
Commit .env.template and .gitignore changes: git add .env.template .gitignore && git commit -m 'chore: audit and update secrets template via basher secrets'

triage_guide.no_template_no_env_files: Project has no secrets. Run basher secrets init to create baseline template, leave empty, commit.
triage_guide.template_exists_no_op_refs: Variables not yet stored in 1Password. Run: basher secrets learn to push values, then basher secrets restore.
triage_guide.template_exists_with_op_refs: Healthy state. Re-run basher secrets init (overwrite) to get latest format, then basher secrets restore to refresh outputs.
triage_guide.env_files_but_no_template: Backwards — secrets are unmanaged. Run basher secrets init (scans existing files), then basher secrets learn to move values to 1Password.
